summary: Added Arcade Deck, a data-driven minigame launcher that turns every lane minigame texture and registry hook into a launchable card with Play, Path, and Asset actions.

pattern_notes: Card grids should use consistent dimensions, clear content hierarchy, and predictable actions so large dashboards remain scannable.
Game-library launchers benefit from visual thumbnails, quick launch actions, and status metadata without turning each card into a dense report.
Minigame selectors should preserve player pacing with direct actions rather than autoplaying or hiding important state inside decorative motion.
The deck reuses existing generated lane textures instead of adding another asset dependency, keeping the standalone static repo fast.

implementation.surface: Top-level Arcade Deck panel between World Loom and Progression Constellation.
data_contract: Classifies custom modules through MINIGAME_REGISTRY via minigameDefinition(lane).
Uses gameStepCount(lane), lane progress, gate counts, lane avatar, realm, and minigame mechanic for card metadata.
Does not require hardcoded lane ids for rendering; future lanes appear after adding texture data.
actions: data-arcade-launch opens selected lane Game view.
data-arcade-path opens selected lane Path Map view.
data-arcade-asset selects the lane and filters Visual Asset Vault to game textures.
visual_contract: Single-column mobile layout and auto-fit desktop card grid.
One active card mirrors selected lane state.
No nested page cards; repeated module cards are the only card layer.

tdd_red.expected_failure: Arcade Deck is not implemented yet

interaction.play: platform_engineering PLAY opens #lane=platform_engineering&view=game with Systems Grid visible.
interaction.path: platform_engineering PATH opens #lane=platform_engineering&view=path with Path Map visible.
interaction.asset: platform_engineering ART filters Asset Vault to game, shows 12 game assets, and keeps the platform asset active.

next_action: Add optional per-lane animated module stingers or short sprite loops on top of Arcade Deck cards while preserving the data-driven static fallback.
